records.ety: [OF. enoint, p. p. of enoindre, fr. L. inungere; in + ungere, unguere, to smear, anoint. See Ointment, Unguent.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Anointed; p. pr. & vb. n. Anointing.]

defs: To smear or rub over with oil or an unctuous substance; also, to spread over, as oil.
quotes: And fragrant oils the stiffened limbs anoint.
He anointed the eyes of the blind man with the clay.

defs: To apply oil to or to pour oil upon, etc., as a sacred rite, especially for consecration.
quotes: Then shalt thou take the anointing oil, and pour it upon his [Aaron's] head and anoint him.
Anoint Hazael to be king over Syria.
